scenes.text:
A leader who cannot admit fault
is a leader nobody trusts.

Accountability starts at the top. When things go wrong, a real leader says my fault. Not my team messed up. Not the market changed. My fault.
This is not weakness. It is the ultimate display of strength. A leader who owns their mistakes earns more respect than one who never makes any.

After you own the mistake, own the fix. Do not just apologize. Show
the solution. Show the lesson learned. Show that failure made the team stronger.

The fastest way to lose trust is to blame others. The
fastest way to earn it is to own your failures.

Have you ever seen a leader own a mistake? How
did it change your respect? Follow for authentic leadership.
